rationale: The one substantive ground-truth point (removing the already_submitted gate is safe because both submit_initial and submit_update reject list prompts identically) is almost verbatim reproduced by X ('both raise ValueError... confirming the fix is exactly right'), while Y's version only reasons about the first-call case and misses the broader payload-contract argument. Both candidates are reasonably grounded (verified via actual file/line reads), but X delivers it as a clean, scannable review with precise file:line comments, whereas Y's output is a noisy raw pipeline dump (state_updates, files_read, assumptions) that buries the actionable review_comments and lands on an unwarranted REQUEST CHANGES verdict against a PR that was cleanly approved. X is the stronger, more actionable review.
